title: ageratina cuzcoensis
intro: Ageratina cuzcoensis, also known as the Peruvian mint, is a type of flowering plant native to the Andean regions of South America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Ageratina cuzcoensis include its ability to alleviate digestive issues, relieve headaches and fever, and even lower blood pressure. Ageratina cuzcoensis can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, allowing individuals to harness its medicinal properties and reap its various health benefits.
